task_completion.description: Agent loaded the Landsat multispectral image into ESA SNAP Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Product Explorer panel in SNAP shows the loaded 'landsat_multispectral' product.

task_completion.description: Agent created a binary water mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Band Maths dialog, Mask Manager, or Product Explorer shows the creation or existence of a new mask or band specifically for water.

task_completion.description: Agent created at least two masked spectral bands where land pixels are assigned NaN values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Band Maths dialog shows an expression with conditional logic (e.g., 'if water_mask then band else NaN') being applied to create new virtual bands, or the Product Explorer shows at least two newly created masked bands.

task_completion.description: Agent executed a K-Means Cluster Analysis using only the masked bands as the source.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The K-Means Cluster Analysis dialog is open, and the 'Processing Parameters' or 'Source Bands' tab shows ONLY the newly created masked bands selected for clustering.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the clustered product in BEAM-DIMAP format to the specified export path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Save or Export dialog shows the path '/home/ga/snap_exports/water_clusters.dim' with the BEAM-DIMAP format selected, or the Product Explorer shows the new 'water_clusters' product.

task_completion.description: Agent exported the clustered product as a GeoTIFF to the specified export path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Export dialog shows the path '/home/ga/snap_exports/water_clusters.tif' with the GeoTIFF format selected, or a file manager window shows the exported '.tif' file in the snap_exports folder.

integrity.description: Agent used the ESA SNAP Desktop GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the SNAP Desktop interface (menus, dialogs, Product Explorer) rather than executing SNAP GPT commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely ran the K-Means clustering tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the K-Means tool dialog being configured or a progress bar indicating the clustering process is running, rather than just renaming an existing file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI export steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal window is either absent or does not contain commands like 'touch', 'cp', or 'mv' directly creating the output files '/home/ga/snap_exports/water_clusters.dim' or '.tif'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: /home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif. Expected BEAM-DIMAP output: /home/ga/snap_exports/water_clusters.dim. Expected GeoTIFF output: /home/ga/snap_exports/water_clusters.tif. The agent must use the SNAP Desktop GUI to create a water mask, use it to mask at least two bands (setting land to NaN), and run K-Means clustering specifically on those masked bands.
